* Merge into `develop` branch after approval

mrs.author: catarina.felipe
mrs.title: feat: Auth0 user synchronization and global notification system implementation
mrs.description:
* **Auth0 User Sync:** Implemented a robust `/api/auth/me` flow that automatically synchronizes Auth0 metadata (name, email, sub) with our local database. It handles user creation on first login and updates existing records if display names are placeholders (e.g., "User" or "New User").
* **Global Notification System:** Implemented backend support for notifications accessible to all users.
  * Added `getNotifications` and `markRead` logic to `siteController`.
  * Ensured notifications are filtered by the user's `auth0Sub`. Bug Fixes & Refactoring


* **Route Priority Fix:** Resolved a critical "NaN" database error (PostgreSQL code `22P02`) by reordering routes in `app.ts`. Static routes like `/api/sites/notifications` are now defined before dynamic routes like `/api/sites/:id` to prevent parameter collision.
* **TypeScript Syntax:** Fixed `TS1005` compilation errors in `siteController.ts` related to method closures and semicolon placement.
* **Data Validation:** Added `Number()` casting and `isNaN` checks for ID parameters to ensure API stability when dealing with database queries.


* Integrated `siteService` more deeply with controllers to separate business logic from routing.
* Improved error handling in the notification flow to return empty states instead of 500 errors, enhancing the frontend user experience.

mrs.author: henrique.diniz
mrs.title: docs: enhance ConfigurationManagement with visual aids and troubleshooting
mrs.description:
## Summary

This MR enhances the ConfigurationManagement.md document with comprehensive improvements to make GitFlow policies clearer, more accessible, and easier to understand for all team members.

## What's New

### 1. GitFlow Visual Diagram (Section 2.1.1)

- Added Mermaid diagram showing complete GitFlow workflow
- Visual representation of feature, release, and hotfix branch flows
- Clear flow summary for quick reference

### 2. Branch Type Decision Tree (Section 2.2.1)

- Interactive decision tree to help choose the correct branch type
- Quick reference guide for common scenarios
- Reduces confusion and mistakes in branch creation

### 3. Semantic Versioning Guide (Section 4)

- Detailed explanation of MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH versioning
- Clear rules for when to increment each component
- Practical examples with real-world scenarios
- Version lifecycle examples
- Tagging workflow procedures

### 4. Troubleshooting Section (Section 8)

- Solutions for merge conflicts
- How to handle outdated branches
- Fixing accidental commits (wrong branch, sensitive data)
- Common Git issues and their solutions (8+ scenarios)
- Emergency recovery procedures

## Impact

- **Lines added:** 437
- **Lines modified:** 17 (section numbering updates only)
- **Information removed:** 0 (all original content preserved)
- **New sections:** 4 major sections + subsections

## Benefits

- Reduces onboarding time for new team members
- Provides self-service troubleshooting resources
- Improves consistency in version management
- Decreases dependency on senior developers for basic Git issues
- Aligns with PMO requirements for complete documentation

## Checklist (DoD)

- [x] Documentation updated and well-structured
- [x] All sections properly formatted
- [x] Practical examples and use cases included
- [x] No breaking changes to existing content
- [x] Follows project documentation standards
- [x] All acceptance criteria met
- [x] Content reviewed for clarity and accuracy

## Acceptance Criteria

:white_check_mark: Updated configuration management policies in ConfigurationManagement.md\
:white_check_mark: Documented and detailed Gitflow branch structure\
:white_check_mark: Defined naming conventions and branch procedures\
:white_check_mark: GitFlow practices aligned with project needs\
:white_check_mark: Practical examples included throughout

## Risk Impact

**No risk impact** - Documentation-only changes. No changes to code, infrastructure, or configurations.

## Related Documentation

- Follows conventions from [Git Flow](https://www.atlassian.com/br/git/tutorials/comparing-workflows/gitflow-workflow)
- Implements [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/) best practices
- Complements existing [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) documentation

mrs.author: nicole.silva
mrs.title: Docs/ Cost Analysis and Effort Estimation
mrs.description:
This PR introduces the **Cost Analysis and Effort Estimation** section to the project documentation, as required by the project management guidelines.

The objective is to simulate the financial effort involved in developing the solution, based on real market data and consistent estimation methodologies.

###  What was done

- Added section **"9. Cost Analysis and Effort Estimation"** to `docs/ProjectManagement.md`
- Calculated total project effort:
  - 7 team members
  - 5 sprints
  - 16 hours per sprint
  - Total: **560 hours**
- Defined a **market-based hourly rate** using industry data (Glassdoor, PayScale, Indeed)
- Estimated total labor cost in:
  - USD
  - BRL (currency conversion included)
- Included:
  - Financial summary table
  - Tax simulation (6%)
  - Cost considerations and risks
- Documented methodology using academic-style justification

This analysis ensures that the project aligns with real-world software development practices by recognizing that even academic projects consume measurable resources. A **conservative estimation approach** was adopted to maintain realism without overestimating costs, especially considering the cross-functional nature of the team.

### Files changed

- `docs/ProjectManagement.md`

###  Notes / Considerations

- No real financial cost is incurred; this is a **simulated estimation**
- Infrastructure costs are currently $0 due to free-tier usage
- Future costs may arise with system scaling

###  How to review

- Verify calculation consistency (hours × rate)
- Check clarity and structure of the documentation
- Ensure alignment with project requirements
